---
title: "Disabling User Mapping"
---

---
title: "Disabling User Mapping"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Content-Federation-Services/gxx1752271911299"
section: "Content Federation Services"
---

The Content Federation Admin Portal allows you to check the status of user mapping for system integrations registered for the Content Federation Services (CFS) application. Then, you may disable user mapping between a system integration and the Content Innovation Cloud (CIC). This may be done to prevent access or to correct the set attributes and then re-enable user mapping.

To disable user mapping:

1.  Log in to the Content Innovation Cloud User Portal. For more information on how to log in to the Content Innovation Cloud User Portal, see Sign-in Options in the Content Innovation Cloud Administration Portal documentation.
2.  Ensure the appropriate environment is selected.
3.  Under Applications, click Content Federation Admin Portal. The Content Federation Connection Manager page is displayed.
4.  Click the User Mapping tab. The User Mapping page is displayed, which shows a list of system integrations and their user mapping status. If a system integration has user mapping enabled, the Last Synchronized column displays when user information was last synchronized between OnBase and the CIC. For system integrations that have user mapping enabled, the Next Synchronization column displays when user information is scheduled to synchronize between OnBase and the CIC.
5.  In the User Mapping column, click the switch to set user mapping from enabled (![User Mapping Switch in the Enabled position](data:image/gif;base64,)) to disabled (![User Mapping Switch in the Disabled position](data:image/gif;base64,)) for the listed system integration. User mapping is disabled, and user information is not synchronized between the system repository and the CIC.
